description: Changing a client default such as timeout, retry count, or base URL in a non-major release, altering behavior for existing users.
body: Failure mode: a default timeout, retry policy, page size, or endpoint is changed in a minor/patch, so consumers who relied on the old behavior see new latency, duplicate calls, or wrong-environment traffic without changing their code. Detection signals: behavior shifts after a non-major upgrade; defaults edited without a changelog entry; tests pinned to old defaults break. Do not change a public default outside a major bump without a deliberate migration note. Safer fallback: treat defaults as semver surface, change them only at a major version with changelog and migration guidance, or make the new behavior opt-in.
